courseleaf.title: Community Nutrition, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The Department of Nutrition offers three minor programs open to students majoring in other disciplines who wish to complement their study programs with a concentration in the area of food and nutrition.

Minor Advisor

Advising for the minor and course offerings is located in the Nutrition Academic Advising Center in 3202 Meyer Hall; 530-752-2512; 530-752-7094.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Note: If the student’s major program requires the same course in biochemistry and physiology, only one of the courses may duplicate credit toward the minor. The program lists replacement courses below which can be used to fulfill the minimum unit requirement.

Course List
Code Title Units
Preparation
Plan in advance to include the required course prerequisites.
NUT 111AYIntroduction to Nutrition & Metabolism3
NUT 111BRecommendations & Standards for Human Nutrition2
NUT 118Community Nutrition4
NUT 192Internship2
NUT 120ANNutritional Anthropology4
or NUT 120BN Nutritional Geography
NPB 101Systemic Physiology5
Replacement Courses
See note, above.
NUT 114
Developmental Nutrition
NUT 116A
Clinical Nutrition
NUT 116AL
Clinical Nutrition Practicum
NUT 116B
Clinical Nutrition
or NUT 116BY
 Clinical Nutrition
NUT 116BL
Clinical Nutrition Practicum
Total Units20
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Community Nutrition minor exposes students to the strategies available to promote proper nutrition among individuals, families and communities. Students analyze the social and environmental influences and available resources that affect food consumption and nutritional status.
